revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Katsuyama-date]][[en:Katsuyama Date]][[ja:勝山館]][[zh:上之國勝山館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11400268]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama-date [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11400268]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama-date [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Castle in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox military installation | name = Katsuyama Date | nativename-a={{lang|ja|勝山館}} | partof = | location = [[:en:Hiyama District, Hokkaido|Hiyama]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | image = Kaminokuni Katuyamadate.jpg | image_size = | caption = Katsuyama Date | type = [[:en:Japanese castle|Yamajiro-style castle]] | height = | ownership = Kakizaki clan | controlledby = | condition = ruins | built = 15c | builder = [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] | used = | materials = | demolished = Unknown | battles = | past_commanders = Takeda Nobuhiro, Kakizaki Mitsuhiro | garrison = | occupants = | events = | coordinates = {{coord|41.8015|140.1007|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Hokkaido#Japan }} {{nihongo|'''Katsuyama Date'''|勝山館|Katsuyama-Date}} is the remains of a castle or fortified residence in [[:en:Hiyama District, Hokkaido|Hiyama]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_01362af2170141646/|title=Katuyama Date|publisher=じゃらん|access-date=13 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2009/04/post-113.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Hakodate Tourism Association|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> It is believed the castle was built by [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] in the 15th century.<ref>{{cite web|URL=http://www.town.kaminokuni.lg.jp/hotnews/detail/00000285.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Kaminokuni Town|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
The castle is now only ruins, just some remnants of moats and earthen walls. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the [[:en:Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan]] in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

{{coord|41.8015|140.1007|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Hokkaido#Japan }} {{nihongo|'''Katsuyama Date'''|勝山館|Katsuyama-Date}} is the remains of a castle or fortified residence in [[:en:Hiyama District, Hokkaido|Hiyama]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_01362af2170141646/|title=Katuyama Date|publisher=じゃらん|access-date=13 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2009/04/post-113.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Hakodate Tourism Association|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> It is believed the castle was built by [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] in the 15th century.<ref>{{cite web|URL=http://www.town.kaminokuni.lg.jp/hotnews/detail/00000285.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Kaminokuni Town|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
The castle is now only ruins, just some remnants of moats and earthen walls. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the [[:en:Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan]] in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

web|URL=https://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2009/04/post-113.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Hakodate Tourism Association|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> It is believed the castle was built by [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] in the 15th century.<ref>{{cite web|URL=http://www.town.kaminokuni.lg.jp/hotnews/detail/00000285.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Kaminokuni Town|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
The castle is now only ruins, just some remnants of moats and earthen walls. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the [[:en:Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan]] in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

caption = Katsuyama Date | type = [[:en:Japanese castle|Yamajiro-style castle]] | height = | ownership = Kakizaki clan | controlledby = | condition = ruins | built = 15c | builder = [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] | used = | materials = | demolished = Unknown | battles = | past_commanders = Takeda Nobuhiro, Kakizaki Mitsuhiro | garrison = | occupants = | events = | coordinates = {{coord|41.8015|140.1007|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Hokkaido#Japan }} {{nihongo|'''Katsuyama Date'''|勝山館|Katsuyama-Date}} is the remains of a castle or fortified residence in [[:en:Hiyama District, Hokkaido|Hiyama]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_01362af2170141646/|title=Katuyama Date|publisher=じゃらん|access-date=13 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2009/04/post-113.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Hakodate Tourism Association|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> It is believed the castle was built by [[:en:Takeda Nobuhiro|Takeda Nobuhiro]] in the 15th century.<ref>{{cite web|URL=http://www.town.kaminokuni.lg.jp/hotnews/detail/00000285.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Kaminokuni Town|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
The castle is now only ruins, just some remnants of moats and earthen walls. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the [[:en:Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan]] in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the {{ill|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|en|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|WD=Q30927700|lt=Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan}} in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama-date]][[en:Katsuyama Date]][[ja:勝山館]][[zh:上之國勝山館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama-date]][[en:Katsuyama Date]][[ja:勝山館]][[zh:上之國勝山館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Katsuyama-date]][[en:Katsuyama Date]][[ja:勝山館]][[zh:上之國勝山館]]
{{short description|Castle in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Infobox military installation | name = Katsuyama Date | nativename-a={{lang|ja|勝山館}} | partof = | location = {{ill|Hiyama District, Hokkaido|en|Hiyama District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1186011|lt=Hiyama|1=Hiyama District, Hokkaido}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | image = Kaminokuni Katuyamadate.jpg | image_size = | caption = Katsuyama Date | type = {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|WD=Q92026|lt=Yamajiro-style castle|1=Japanese castle}} | height = | ownership = Kakizaki clan | controlledby = | condition = ruins | built = 15c | builder = {{ill|Takeda Nobuhiro|en|Takeda Nobuhiro|WD=Q7677963|lt=Takeda Nobuhiro|1=Takeda Nobuhiro}} | used = | materials = | demolished = Unknown | battles = | past_commanders = Takeda Nobuhiro, Kakizaki Mitsuhiro | garrison = | occupants = | events = | coordinates = {{coord|41.8015|140.1007|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Hokkaido#Japan }} {{nihongo|'''Katsuyama Date'''|勝山館|Katsuyama-Date}} is the remains of a castle or fortified residence in {{ill|Hiyama District, Hokkaido|en|Hiyama District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1186011|lt=Hiyama|1=Hiyama District, Hokkaido}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.jalan.net/kankou/spt_01362af2170141646/|title=Katuyama Date|publisher=じゃらん|access-date=13 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2009/04/post-113.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Hakodate Tourism Association|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> It is believed the castle was built by {{ill|Takeda Nobuhiro|en|Takeda Nobuhiro|WD=Q7677963|lt=Takeda Nobuhiro|1=Takeda Nobuhiro}} in the 15th century.<ref>{{cite web|URL=http://www.town.kaminokuni.lg.jp/hotnews/detail/00000285.html|title=Katsuyama Date|publisher=Kaminokuni Town|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
The castle is now only ruins, just some remnants of moats and earthen walls. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site, since 1977.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/54|title=上之国館跡 花沢館跡 洲崎館跡 勝山館跡|publisher=文化庁 [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref> Katsuyama Date was listed as one of the {{ill|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|en|Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|WD=Q30927700|lt=Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan|1=Continued 100 Fine Castles of Japan}} in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|URL= http://jokaku.jp/japan-top-100-castles/best-100-castles-of-japan-2nd-selection/ |title=続日本100名城 |publisher=日本城郭協会|language=Japanese|access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
Guidance facility of the castle is on site.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://www.hiyama.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/ss/srk/tabi/09kamino/parts/3903gds.html|title=勝山館跡|publisher=Hokkaido |access-date=14 October 2021}}</ref>
